steps.title: Spot the main verb that governs the structure
steps.content: The sentence contains “adapted the test,” a clear past tense finite verb expressing the primary action. Its position signals that the blank cannot contain another finite verb.

steps.title: Match the verb form to the sentence structure
steps.content:
The participle “searching” creates a modifying phrase for “scientists” and establishes “adapted the test” as the sentence's sole main verb.

Using any finite verb (searched, were searching, have searched) would place two main verbs in a sequence that requires a conjunction like “and” or a semicolon to be properly joined—neither of which appears here.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
